LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL GIEDGOWD'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED IN LARGE PART. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED ATTORNEY'S FEES OF $166,667, REPRESENTING ONE-THIRD OF THE JUDGMENT AMOUNT. WHILE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ESTIMATES THAT HE SPENT OVER 500 OURS ON THIS CASE, HIS RECONSTRUCTED BILLING RECORDS SHOW ONLY 393.5 HOURS. MOREOVER, WHILE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SAYS THAT HIS HOURLY RATE EXCEEDS $500, HE PROVIDES NO INFORMATION HOW THAT NUMBER WAS ARRIVED AT OR THAT IT HAS BEEN CHARGED TO ANY CLIENT AND HE STATED TWO YEARS AGO THAT HIS HOURLY RATE EXCEEDS $300. AN HOURLY RATE OF $400 MULTIPLIED BY 400 HOURS YIELDS $160,000, WHICH IS IN THE VICINITY OF A ONE-THIRD CONTINGENCY FEE. NO COSTS ARE AWARDED. RECOVERABLE COSTS WERE DETERMINED AT THE APRIL 5, 2012, HEARING ON THE MOTION TO TAX. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED. =(302/HEK)

DISCOVERY 301, A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT KEVIN MCGUINNESS' MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING THE DEPOSITION OF EXPERT WITNESS DR. PETER GODSICK - DEFENDANT MUST PRODUCE THE WITNESS IN SAN FRANCISCO BUT, IN ORDER TO AVOID UNNECESSARY BURDEN, THE DEPOSITION MAY TAKE PLACE 2 DAYS PRIOR TO THE WITNESS' APPEARANCE TO TESTIFY AT TRIAL SO LONG AS THE WITNESS PROVIDES A WRITTEN REPORT SUMMARIZING HIS FINDINGS NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 25, 2011. IF DR. GODSICK WILL NOT APPEAR AT THE TRIAL THERE IS NO NEED FOR HIM TO APPEAR IN SAN FRANCISCO. MR. MUSFELT TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT KEVIN MCGUINNESS' MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED AS TO THE CLAIMED COSTS IN ITEMS 5 AND 8 AND DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER COSTS THAT HAVE BEEN CHALLENGED. AS TO ITEM 5, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT COUNSEL WAS SERVED WITH PROCESS. THE LACK OF MERIT TO ITEM 8 IS CONCEDED. ALL OTHER COSTS ARE PROPER. A TOTAL OF $519.00 IS STRICKEN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Plaintiff Michael Giedgowd'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jun-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - KRISTINE MEREDIAN OF DANKO LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (650) 342-6100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Michael Giedgowd'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs is Granted in large part. Plaintiff is awarded attorney's fees of $166,667, representing one-third of the judgment amount. While plaintiff's counsel estimates that he spent over 500 ours on this case, his reconstructed billing records show only 393.5 hours. Moreover, while plaintiff's counsel says that his hourly rate exceeds $500, he provides no information how that number was arrived at or that it has been charged to any client and he stated two years ago that his hourly rate exceeds $300. An hourly rate of $400 multiplied by 400 hours yields $160,000, which is in the vicinity of a one-third contingency fee. No costs are awarded. Recoverable costs were determined at the April 5, 2012, hearing on the motion to tax. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. =(302/HEK)

Plaintiff Michael Giedgowd'S Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs

Mtn To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Apr-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAUREN KOBLITZ OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 438-5937 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KEVIN MCGUINNESS' Motion To Tax Costs is Granted as to the claimed costs in items 5 and 8 and denied as to all other costs that have been challenged. As to item 5, there is no showing that counsel was served with process. The lack of merit to item 8 is conceded. All other costs are proper. A total of $519.00 is stricken. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)

ATTY. DUANE C. MUSFELT OF THE LEWIS, BRISBOIS, BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 438-6606 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT KEVIN MCGUINNESS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT KEVIN MCGUINNESS' Motion For Protective Order regarding the deposition of expert witness Dr. Peter Godsick - Defendant must produce the witness in San Francisco but, in order to avoid unnecessary burden, the deposition may take place 2 days prior to the witness' appearance to testify at trial so long as the witness provides a written report summarizing his findings no later than February 25, 2011.
